title: Tungi fort
tagline: Tungi fort — heritage city of Maharashtra

overview.short: Tungi fort (Marathi: तुंगी किल्ला) is a small 16th-century fort probably mainly used as a lookout post in the past.
overview.description: Tungi fort (Marathi: तुंगी किल्ला) is a small 16th-century fort probably mainly used as a lookout post in the past. It is situated to the east of Karjat in the Indian state of Maharashtra. It is one of the easy-to-reach forts near the Bhimashanker trek route in the Karjat area.

nearestAirport.name: Chhatrapati Shivaji Maharaj International Airport, Mumbai

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Ambivali Caves
topPlaces.description: The Ambivali Caves, or Ambivali Leni, are a group of Buddhist caves near Neral, Raigad district, Maharashtra, 8km southeast of Kalyan. The caves are cut in the low hill located on the concave portion of a river.

topPlaces.name: Bhimashankar Temple
topPlaces.description: Bhimashankar Temple is a Hindu temple dedicated to Shiva situated in its eponymous village, Bhimashankar, in Pune district of Maharashtra. It is a key pilgrimage centre and contains one of the 12 Jyotirlingas.

topPlaces.name: Kashele
topPlaces.description: Kashele is a village near the city of Karjat, in the state of Maharashtra, India. The village is located 82 km from Mumbai, and its official language is Marathi.

topPlaces.name: Ambegaon, Mawal
topPlaces.description: Ambegaon is a village and gram panchayat in India, situated in Mawal taluka of Pune district in the state of Maharashtra. It encompasses an area of 288 ha (712 acres).

items.note: The Ambivali Caves, or Ambivali Leni, are a group of Buddhist caves near Neral, Raigad district, Maharashtra, 8km southeast of Kalyan. The caves are cut in the low hill located on the concave portion of a river.

Bhimashankar Temple is a Hindu temple dedicated to Shiva situated in its eponymous village, Bhimashankar, in Pune district of Maharashtra. It is a key pilgrimage centre and contains one of the 12 Jyotirlingas.
Kashele is a village near the city of Karjat, in the state of Maharashtra, India. The village is located 82 km from Mumbai, and its official language is Marathi.
Ambegaon is a village and gram panchayat in India, situated in Mawal taluka of Pune district in the state of Maharashtra. It encompasses an area of 288 ha (712 acres).

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Tungi fort?
faq.a: The best time to visit Tungi fort is Oct – Mar. Summers are 26–40°C and winters are 12–28°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Tungi fort?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Chhatrapati Shivaji Maharaj International Airport, Mumbai (66 km) and the nearest railway station is Kalyan Junction (Mumbai) (44 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Tungi fort?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Tungi fort at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Tungi fort?
faq.a: Stays in Tungi fort start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Tungi fort?
faq.a: The top attractions are Ambivali Caves, Bhimashankar Temple, Kashele, Ambegaon, Mawal.
